title: Claude Code Automation: Screen-clicking vs MCP
voiceScript: Everyone's excited that Claude Code can now click through any app like a human. That's the part I trust least. Screen clicking looks like magic in a demo, but it breaks the moment a button moves, and the whole flow falls apart mid-run. The durable wins come from MCP. Structured read and write straight into your real tools. Your inbox, your CRM, your sheets. That's the difference between a magic trick and infrastructure. So now I ask one thing. Does this tool expose a real connection, or am I asking an agent to pretend to be a mouse? Start with one boring, repetitive task. Prove it, then expand. That's the only approach that compounds. What would you automate first? Follow for more.
source: LinkedIn post: Claude Code Computer Use (visual screen-clicking) is fragile and breaks on any layout change mid-run, useful only for legacy portals with no API. The durable wins come from MCP: structured read/write into real tools (inbox, CRM, sheets, accounting). Screen-scraping is a magic trick; calling an API is infrastructure. Scope rule: does the tool expose a real connection, or are you asking an agent to pretend to be a mouse? If mouse, price in the breakage. Winners pick one boring repetitive task, prove it against edge cases, then expand. Start small is the only approach that compounds.
